name: Canada
description: The Great White North — history, geography, and culture

questions.q: In which month is Canada Day celebrated each year?

source.notes: July 1, commemorating the 1867 Constitution Act that united three colonies.

questions.q: Which sport is widely considered Canada's official winter national sport?

source.notes: Lacrosse is the national summer sport; hockey the national winter sport per 1994 legislation.

questions.q: What is the capital city of Canada?

source.notes: Located in Ontario, on the Ottawa River.

questions.q: What leaf appears on Canada's national flag?

source.notes: The red maple leaf flag was adopted in February 1965.

questions.q: Which province joined Canadian Confederation in 1949 as its tenth province?

source.notes: Renamed 'Newfoundland and Labrador' in 2001.

questions.q: Who became Prime Minister of Canada in November 2015?

source.notes: Son of former PM Pierre Trudeau.

questions.q: What is the nickname of Canada's one-dollar coin, featuring a common loon on its reverse?

source.notes: Introduced in 1987; the $2 coin became the 'toonie' in 1996.

questions.q: Who is the all-time leading goal scorer in NHL history?

source.notes: 894 career goals; broken only by Alex Ovechkin's career total pace as of mid-2020s.

questions.q: Which Scottish-born Canadian inventor is credited with patenting the telephone in 1876?

source.notes: Conducted early telephone experiments in Brantford, Ontario.

questions.q: The CN Tower, once the world's tallest free-standing structure, is located in which Canadian city?

source.notes: Completed 1976; held the tallest-free-standing record until 2007 (Burj Khalifa surpassed it).

questions.q: Which Canadian territory was created in 1999, carved out of the Northwest Territories?

source.notes: Means 'our land' in Inuktitut; mostly Inuit population.

questions.q: What was the original name of the Canadian holiday now known as 'Canada Day'?

source.notes: Renamed in 1982; Dominion Day marked confederation July 1, 1867.

questions.q: Which Canadian province has French as its sole official language?

source.notes: New Brunswick is officially bilingual; the rest are officially English.

questions.q: Which Canadian city hosted the 2010 Winter Olympics?

source.notes: Calgary had hosted the 1988 Winter Games; Montreal the 1976 Summer Games.

questions.q: Who became Canada's first female Prime Minister in 1993?

source.notes: Served from June to November 1993 as leader of the Progressive Conservatives.

questions.q: Along which latitude does most of the border between Canada and the contiguous United States run?

source.notes: The 'straight-line' western section of the border follows the 49th parallel north.

questions.q: Cirque du Soleil, the globally famous circus company, was founded in which Canadian province in 1984?

source.notes: Founded in Baie-Saint-Paul, Quebec by Guy Laliberté and Gilles Ste-Croix.

questions.q: The board game 'Trivial Pursuit', launched in 1981, was invented by two Canadians in which year?

source.notes: Chris Haney and Scott Abbott conceived the game in Montreal in December 1979.

questions.q: On D-Day in 1944, Canadian forces landed on which code-named beach?

source.notes: Canadian 3rd Infantry Division landed at Juno on June 6, 1944.

questions.q: The 1896 Klondike Gold Rush primarily took place in which Canadian territory?

source.notes: Gold was found in a tributary of the Klondike River near Dawson City.

questions.q: Which Canadian province became the first to grant women the right to vote, in 1916?

source.notes: Manitoba granted the provincial vote in January 1916, followed by Saskatchewan and Alberta.

questions.q: Chief Tecumseh, a key ally of the British in the War of 1812, was the leader of which First Nation?

source.notes: Killed at the Battle of the Thames in October 1813.
